prompt: The reflective method invocation throws InvocationTargetException. What is the bug?
choices: getDeclaredMethod cannot find static methods; use getMethod instead

invoke throws InvocationTargetException wrapping NullPointerException because greet concatenates null which actually works — the real bug is m.invoke(null, (Object) null) passes null as the name argument, not null for the instance, and greet is static so null instance is correct
The method throws NullPointerException inside greet because null is passed as name, which is wrapped in InvocationTargetException; use an empty string instead of null
Static methods cannot be called via Method.invoke()
explanation: m.invoke(null, null) passes null as the name argument to greet(). Inside greet, "Hello " + null yields "Hello null" — actually no NPE there. The real scenario: InvocationTargetException wraps any exception thrown inside the invoked method. If greet itself throws (e.g. name.length()), that surfaces as InvocationTargetException. The fix is to pass a valid String: m.invoke(null, "World").
